channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: HashiCorp built an MCP server for writing Terraform. I built one for reviewing it
item.description: HashiCorp built an MCP server for writing Terraform. I built one for reviewing it. A few weeks ago HashiCorp shipped terraform-mcp-server. It's an official MCP server that lets a model lean on the...

item.title: Why Enterprise AI Agent Deployments Keep Failing
item.description: In 2026, the gap between AI agent demos and AI agent deployments is the most expensive gap in enterprise technology. We've watched organizations spin up promising pilots, hit a wall somewhere...

item.title: Date Shear: A New Term for a Common Programming Pain Point
item.description: One of the sneakiest, hardest to explain bugs. The process works fine any day you test it, but occasionally - and only occasionally it's just... wrong. Test it again and it's fine, no matter what...

item.title: Compass v1.1.0 · we shipped a memory plugin that catches its own consumption drift
item.description: Compass v1.1.0 · the recall consumption fix We shipped nautilus-compass v1.1.0 12 hours after v1.0.0. v1.0.0 was the public stable cut. v1.1.0 fixes a class of failure that v1.0.0 surfaces but...

item.title: Zod Validation: Type-Safe APIs & Forms in TypeScript (Complete Guide)
item.description: TypeScript types disappear at runtime. Your carefully typed User interface means nothing when the JSON from an external API, a form submission, or a database query arrives as unknown. Zod bridges...

item.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD: Build a Complete Node.js Pipeline (2026)
item.description: A CI/CD pipeline that catches bugs before deploy, builds reproducible Docker images, and ships to production with zero downtime is table stakes for any serious Node.js project. GitHub Actions...

item.title: MCP in 2026: The numbers behind the ecosystem explosion
item.description: I spent an afternoon digging through the MCP ecosystem numbers. Here is what I found. The numbers 13,000+ MCP servers on npm and GitHub (as of May 2026) 97 million monthly SDK downloads — that is...

item.title: Beyond the engine: 10 open source projects shaping how games actually get made
item.description: Check out these 10 open source tools that help game developers create art, animation, levels, audio, dialogue, debug UIs, and engine-ready assets. The post Beyond the engine: 10 open source...

item.title: Amazon, Facebook, FBI have access to a private intelligence-sharing network
item.description: Article URL: https://prismreports.org/2026/05/20/seattle-shield-private-companies-surveillance/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48226588 Points: 41 # Comments: 4

item.title: working with an ai model mirror
item.description: thesis working with a fast artificial intelligence model can feel like looking in a mirror. when i recently used gemini 3.5 flash for codebase maintenance and deployment improvements, i found a...

item.title: Bungie gives up on Destiny
item.description: Bungie is moving on from Destiny 2. The studio announced Thursday that it's releasing the last major update for the game on June 9th as its focus "turns towards a new beginning for Bungie." After...

item.title: Trump delays AI security executive order: ‘I don’t want to get in the way of that leading’
item.description: President Trump delayed signing an executive order that would have required pre-release government security reviews of AI models, citing dissatisfaction with the order's language.

item.title: Sonos’s pint-sized Roam 2 speaker is 25 percent off for Memorial Day
item.description: If your summer plans involve spending any time outdoors, whether on the trail or at a pool party, a good portable Bluetooth speaker can go a long way. The latest Sonos Roam is built precisely for...

item.title: The Endless AI guitar pedal has potential
item.description: I'm not sure anyone was really asking for an AI guitar pedal. But it was inevitable that someone would build one. One of the first to take the plunge is Polyend, a well-respected music gear maker...

item.title: Uber is deploying its own self-driving cars again, just not as robotaxis
item.description: Uber is putting its own autonomous vehicles back on the road as part of its new AV Lab project to collect data for its dozens of robotaxi partners. The cars will be fitted with all the sensors...

item.title: This mechanical bird drops dead when your home’s air quality worsens
item.description: The original Birdie debuted in 2022 with a morbid-but-fun design - an air-quality monitor with a literal canary in a coal mine. When the CO2 in your home hit a certain level, a mechanical bird...

item.title: The cost of the smart home is going up
item.description: Selling the smart home has been hard. Even Amazon has lost money in the space, despite putting hundreds of millions of Echo devices in people's homes. Google has also reportedly struggled to turn...

item.title: macOS Kernel Memory Corruption Exploit
item.description: A group used Anthropic’s Mythos AI model to help find a kernel memory corruption vulnerability and exploit on Apple’s M5. News article.

item.title: Meta and Google get data from the app your boss uses to track you
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of workplaces use software to monitor employees. Now, a new study has found that many of these tools share data not just with employers, but with digital advertising...

item.title: GitHub faces a fight for its survival at Microsoft
item.description: When Microsoft announced it was acquiring GitHub in a $7.5 billion deal in 2018, developers were nervous. Some were concerned about Microsoft controlling GitHub, and others were taking a...

item.title: Spotify is launching AI-generated remixes
item.description: Spotify and Universal Music Group (UMG) just announced a licensing deal that will allow users to prompt the creation of AI-generated remixes and covers for streaming songs. The tool will be a paid...

item.title: I Cloned Myself With Gemini’s AI Avatar Tool. The Result Was Unnervingly Me
item.description: I used the Gemini app to generate lifelike videos featuring a digital clone of myself. Google sees this as the future of creation. I’m still creeped out.

item.title: Spotify is going to reserve concert tickets for ‘real fans’
item.description: As part of a new "Reserved" program, Spotify is going to start offering concert tickets for "real fans" of participating bands that are going on tour. The idea with the Reserved program is to give...

item.title: Spotify launches an ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation tool
item.description: Spotify is releasing new audiobook plans later this year

item.title: Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing generation features to podcasts
item.description: Spotify will let you generate daily or weekly briefs based on your prompts

item.title: Spotify takes on Google’s NotebookLM with its new app
item.description: Spotify is releasing the new desktop app as a research preview in more than 20 markets.

item.title: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
item.description: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a...

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at...

item.title: Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive ‘universal’ AI interface
item.description: Hark expects to release its first multimodal models this summer, which it says will power a personal AI platform that works with existing products and services. The company expects to follow that...

item.title: The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
item.description: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots.
